reasoning: With only roughly two months until the January 1, 2026 deadline, the probability of the U.S. enacting substantive AI regulatory legislation is virtually zero. The current administration under President Trump has prioritized deregulation, evidenced by the repeal of the Biden-era AI Executive Order to "remove barriers." Congress is currently focused on preempting state-level AI regulations rather than creating a federal regulatory framework, as seen with H.R. 5388, which explicitly restricts federal agencies from imposing substantive design or performance requirements on AI. Given the extremely tight timeline, the lack of any comprehensive regulatory bills nearing a vote, the anti-regulatory stance of the incumbent administration, and the crowd consensus of 1%, the likelihood of such legislation passing is at the lowest permissible floor.
batch_queries: Will the U.S. federal government enact legislation that substantively regulates or authorizes regulation of artificial intelligence systems or their development before 1 January 2026?
